headline: Coca-Cola's $6 Billion Tax Fight: How Transfer Pricing Works
summary: In 2020, a US Tax Court largely upheld the IRS's transfer pricing adjustments against Coca-Cola. That left the company facing about $2.7 billion in additional taxes after the court found the company had under-reported income from transactions between its overseas affiliates. With interest, the total swelled to roughly $6 billion. Coke is appealing the decision. Cross-border transfer pricing is how multinational companies price transactions between related entities. Governments use these rules to prevent improper profit shifting. This video explains how it works and how it can trigger billion-dollar tax bills.

headline: Keenfolks Launches KEENFOLKS X_

headline: Coca-Cola (KO) Valuation Check As Sprite Returns To NBA And Leadership Transition Takes Shape
summary: Coca-Cola (KO) is back at center court after Sprite reclaimed the NBA soft drink sponsorship, while a CEO handover to Henrique Braun and fresh product launches spotlight how management is repositioning the beverage giant. See our latest analysis for Coca-Cola. Despite all the headlines around Sprite’s NBA return, leadership changes and new product launches, Coca-Cola’s recent share price has cooled, with a 1-month share price return of a 3.44% decline after a 90-day gain of 7.97% and a 1-year...
